Role: Senior Finance Manager, Pune
Experience: Qualified CA with 4+ years
Salary: Up to 18 LPA

Note: CA qualification is mandatory

___________________________________________

About the Role:

The Senior Finance Manager will work directly with the CFO Consultant and COO, managing a team of 5 members across multiple locations.

With the organization's growth plans, there is a need to further strengthen existing financial systems through robust monitoring, evaluation frameworks, and process improvements.

We are looking for someone with strong technical expertise who is eager to enhance systems and processes, combining best practices with contextual innovation.

Key Responsibilities

1. Team & Stakeholder Management
• Manage and build capacity of the Finance team
• Maintain and strengthen relationships with partner organizations and funding partners
• Act as the single point of contact for finance-related queries from funders
• Review government tenders from a compliance and implementation perspective
• Liaise with internal teams (Leadership, HR, Fundraising, Communications, Program teams)
• Coordinate with external stakeholders (auditors, donors, banks, CS, PF & ESIC consultants, etc.)

2. Program Budgeting & Financial Planning
• Prepare organizational budgets in collaboration with stakeholders and track variances
• Review donor utilization reports and optimize fund usage
• Evaluate donor proposals based on budgets and compliance requirements
• Track cash flows and prepare quarterly MIS and financial reports
• Conduct quarterly reviews with project leaders on fund utilization
• Develop and update SOPs for streamlined processes
• Manage and authorize expenses in line with approved budgets
• Review agreements from a financial perspective

3. Compliance & Financial Reporting
• Oversee statutory compliances (FCRA, TDS, PT, Income Tax, GST, etc.)
• Review monthly payroll
• Review financial statements and books of accounts
• Manage internal and external audits (CA & donor audits)
• Prepare quarterly board reports and periodic financial reports
• Set up finance systems in new locations as the organization expands
• Continuously improve accounting and reporting systems

Qualifications, Experience & Key Competencies
• Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) with 4+ years of post-articleship experience in accounts, taxation, and compliance
• Strong knowledge of Income Tax, TDS, PT, and GST
• Experience in budgeting, cash flow management, and MIS (preferred)
• Prior experience in the development/NGO sector (preferred)
• Familiarity with FCRA, PF, and ESIC compliances (preferred)
• Strong leadership and team management capabilities
• Proficiency in Excel and Tally
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with strong written and spoken English
• Ability to work independently, take ownership, and solve problems effectively.

-

At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.

Position: Manager – GL Close/FP&A/Investment Accounting/Reg Reporting/Financial Accounting

Experience: 10+ Years

Overview:

We are seeking an experienced Manager with 8-10 years of progressive accounting and financial planning experience to lead financial operations and financial transformation projects. The successful candidate will oversee general ledger close processes, month-end accounting operations, investment accounting, and financial planning activities. The Manager will lead finance transformation initiatives focused on General Ledger (GL) Close, FP&A, and Record to Report operations within Financial Services firms. This role requires a strategic manager with extensive process expertise and proficiency in technology platforms (e.g., Oracle, Workday, Adaptive, PowerApps) to drive significant value. The Manager will leverage process improvement methodologies and technology as enablers to achieve operational efficiency and enhance accuracy through best-in-class processes and tools.

Key Responsibilities:
• Chart of Accounts Mapping & Redesign: Design, implement, optimize Chart of Accounts structures that align with reporting requirements. Lead CoA mapping during ERP implementations, ensuring seamless data translation. Develop dimensional hierarchies and account coding standards to support multi-entity consolidation and reporting.
• Management of GL Close Activities: Lead and manage the complete GL close and month-end accounting cycles, ensuring timely, accurate, and compliant financial statement preparation. Review journal entries, reconciliation procedures and account analyses to ensure financial statement integrity. Coordinate inter-company eliminations, consolidation adjustments and balance sheet reconciliations.
• Investment Accounting Operations Oversee investment accounting operations, including security transactions, valuations, reconciliations, and reporting. Prepare investment performance reports, reconcile investment holdings and manage regulatory compliance for investment portfolios.
• Transformation Initiatives: Support strategic finance transformation programs, including ERP implementations, system migrations, and digital process redesign.
• Comprehensive As-Is Assessments: Conduct detailed assessments of current Finance processes, including planning, budgeting, reporting, and finance close, utilize process mapping and modelling tools viz. Visio, ARIS and identify areas for improvement.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including IT, Tax, Treasury, and Operations, to drive integrated finance transformation and foster a culture of continuous improvement.
• Technology Transformation: Establish automated close processes and eliminate legacy systems through process redesign. Implement advanced accounting platforms viz. Workday, Workiva, Blackline, Tagetik, Trintech and spearhead finance transformation initiatives.

Key Skills:
• GL Close: General Ledger Management, Chart of Accounts Design and Month end close processes
• Investment Accounting: Strong experience in Investment Accounting, NAV calculations and securities management with valuation expertise
• Deep Understanding of FP&A Processes: Extensive knowledge of FP&A processes, including forecasting, budgeting, finance close, intercompany accounting, financial reporting standards, and management reporting.
• Finance Transformation: Proven experience in conducting As-Is and To-Be process assessments, process redesign and continuous improvement methodologies.
• Finance Systems Proficiency: Significant experience with Finance Systems such as Oracle EPM, Anaplan, Adaptive, Workiva, Workday, Blackline, Trintech, Tagetik and designing operating models that integrate people, processes, and technology.
• Stakeholder Engagement: Demonstrated experience in stakeholder engagement, facilitating workshops, and translating complex business needs into actionable solutions.

Education:
• Domain Expertise: Strong exposure to working in the Financial Services domain across Banking, Insurance, Asset Management, and Capital Markets.
• Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration. Advanced degrees (MBA or equivalent) are preferred. B.Tech or Engineering graduates with relevant work experience in finance or financial systems.

Experience Requirements:
• Extensive Experience: 10+ years of experience in finance operations, accounting, or transformation roles.
• Proven Track Record: A successful history of driving finance process improvements, optimizing close cycles, implementing automation, and ensuring compliance in GL Close, FP&A, and R2R functions.

EY | Building a better working world

EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets.

Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate.

Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

The successful candidate will drive financial excellence, strategic planning, and business growth through advanced financial modeling, compliance management, and cross-functional leadership.

data.job_description:
Senior Accountant - Lease
The Opportunity:
Assist in the preparation of external and internal financial reporting including all periodic Securities & Exchange Commission (“SEC”) filings. Work closely with different functions including Corporate Consolidations, Global Finance, Legal, Tax, Treasury and internal and external auditors. Facilitate the information flow from all subsidiaries and the process of preparing SEC filings. Assist with improvements to financial reporting processes.

This role is responsible for supporting core corporate accounting activities, including lease accounting under ASC 842, stock‑based compensation accounting under ASC 718, monthly close processes, and coordination with global teams. The position requires strong analytical abilities, attention to detail, and effective cross‑functional collaboration.
Major Responsibilities
Lease Accounting & Fixed Assets
• Enter new leases and update lease changes in the Lease Query Tool.
• Prepare and record monthly journal entries and payment reconciliations in alignment with ASC 842.
• Collaborate with global teams to support centralized lease accounting processes.
Stock‑Based Compensation
• Prepare and analyze stock‑based compensation expenses in accordance with ASC 718.
• Support quarterly disclosure requirements for 10Q/10K filings.
Month-End Close & Reporting
• Support the monthly close process, including preparation and review of journal entries, account reconciliations, analytical reviews, and documentation.
• Prepare monthly results for reporting into the data mapping tool for consolidated financials.
• Assist Financial Reporting and Consolidation teams with close activities and data needs.
Business Partnership & Collaboration
• Build strong working relationships with finance leaders and cross‑functional teams.
• Develop a solid understanding of financial processes, inter‑department data flows, reporting needs, and ongoing initiatives.
• Communicate insights clearly and responsibly to the Controller’s Group.
Audit Support
• Support quarterly internal and external audit processes with accurate, compliant documentation.
Process Improvement
• Continuously evaluate existing processes to identify opportunities to improve efficiency, accessibility of financial data, and data integrity.
Ad‑hoc Support
• Perform ad‑hoc financial and economic analyses as requested.
• Contribute to special projects and other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
Education & Experience
• CA Intermediate with 3–6 years of relevant experience, or
• CA with 1–2 years of experience in Record to Report
• Demonstrated experience with technical accounting topics such as Lease Accounting and Stock‑Based Compensation.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel required; experience with SAP is mandatory.
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
• Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP, IFRS, and corporate accounting policies.
• Familiarity with Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) requirements, with experience applying relevant controls.
• Ability to prepare, interpret, and analyze financial statements, including P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills to effectively collaborate with multiple teams and stakeholders.
• Strong analytical mindset, organizational skills, and the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks.
• Proactive, detail‑oriented, and committed to continuous learning.

Role Overview

This is a unique opportunity to join the India Shared Services Centre of one of the UK's fastest-growing private healthcare groups.

As a Financial Analyst – Corporate Finance, you will drive high-impact strategic and financial initiatives ranging from capital structuring and fundraising to M&A and growth planning. You'll work closely with the UK Head of Corporate Finance and collaborate across business units and support teams.

While this is a full-time, in-office role at our Pune Shared Services Centre, your reporting manager is based in the UK. You will therefore be expected to work with a high degree of independence, manage your own priorities, and communicate proactively with senior stakeholders across time zones.

This role is ideal for someone with a strong technical foundation in finance, a commercial mindset, and the ambition to scale with a global organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Corporate Finance & Investment Strategy
• Advise on and support external corporate finance transactions including equity and debt fundraising, property financing, and M&A.
• Conduct business case evaluations using DCF, IRR, and sensitivity models for strategic investment decisions.
• Build and maintain advanced financial models to assess capital deployment, returns, and valuation.
• Prepare investor-grade presentations and reports for the Board, investors, and senior stakeholders.
• Support exit preparedness and stakeholder communications in future financing rounds.

FP&A Support & Business Partnering
• Work closely with the FP&A team to track centre-level profitability and margin expansion initiatives.
• Act as a finance partner to UK centres, supporting cost initiatives, pricing analysis, and strategic planning.
• Assist in the development of dashboards, KPIs, and unit economics frameworks for operational improvement.
• Prepare monthly performance reviews and collaborate with clinical and commercial teams to enhance financial visibility.

Strategic Initiatives
• Contribute to cross-functional strategic projects, including service expansion, new partnerships, and geographic growth.
• Bring analytical insight to complex financial problems involving multiple stakeholders.

What Success Looks Like in Six Months
• Independently handling UK stakeholder requests.
• Delivering 2–3 high-quality financial models or analyses.
• Contributing to at least one strategic initiative (M&A / expansion / fundraising).

Skills & Experience Required

Must-Have
• 4–8 years of experience in corporate finance, corporate development, investment banking, FP&A, or consulting
• Strong command over financial modeling, valuation, and capital structuring concepts
• Excellent written and verbal communication, including ability to create polished presentations and reports
• Highly organized with strong analytical and commercial acumen
• Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent priorities and deliver under tight timelines
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills
• A self-starter with intellectual curiosity and a commercial mindset, comfortable operating in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment
• A CA, CA (Inter), or MBA (Finance) qualification is preferred, though not mandatory for exceptionally strong candidates.

Preferred
• Healthcare sector experience is strongly preferred
• Prior exposure to private equity, or services industries
• Hands-on experience working with UK/Europe-based clients
• Knowledge of group financial reporting, FP&A tools (e.g., Power BI, Anaplan)

Working Conditions
• Office environment in Pune (WFO).
• Standard corporate workweek, with occasional extended hours during deal closings or month-end reporting.
• Collaborative team setting; regular interaction with global finance colleagues and cross-functional business units.

Please answer the qualifying questions carefully — incomplete or casual responses may lead to your application not being considered.

If you’re ready to take ownership, sharpen your expertise, and make a real impact in global healthcare finance, apply today and grow with us!

We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.
